mnemonics: Use the S.P.L.I.T. method: S - Start with official course materials. P - Practice with online platforms (LeetCode, etc.). L - Look for visualizations (YouTube, Animations). I - Implement code yourself. T - Test your understanding with peers.
keyPoints: Official university materials (slides, textbooks) are the primary and most reliable resource.
Online coding platforms (LeetCode, HackerRank) provide essential practical application and problem-solving practice.
Visual learning aids (animations, videos) are crucial for understanding complex data structure operations and traversal.
Active implementation (writing code from scratch) is the best way to achieve deep, lasting understanding.
Peer discussion and group study help solidify knowledge and expose gaps in understanding.
commonMistakes: Relying solely on one type of resource (e.g., only watching videos without coding).
Passively consuming content instead of actively implementing the data structures.
Using advanced or niche resources that may not align with the specific syllabus or learning outcomes of your module.
